Roll out of bed and head straight to the theater for the SUPER MARIO GALAXY Cereal + Pajama Party. Show up in your coziest PJs and get ready to go wild on an all-you-can-eat cereal buffet (yes, really). No additional purchase necessary – your ticket includes all the cereal you can eat.

show.title: High Plains Drifter
show.headline: They'd never forget the day he drifted into town

event.title: <show-title>High Plains Drifter</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Bob Odenkirk and Ben Wheatley, ahead of their new film NORMAL
event.description:
HIGH PLAINS DRIFTER is presented as part of our Bob Odenkirk and Ben Wheatley Guest Selects for their new film, NORMAL.

HIGH PLAINS DRIFTER is one of the great old Clint Eastwood Westerns. It's got a lot of bad feelings in it, and yet somehow, I related to it as a kid in Chicago watching it on TV. Man, I love this movie. It's paced a little...I'll use the word slow – do you know what that word means? And it's still great. It's got a twinkle in its grimy eye. There was a moment in the movie where Ben [Wheatley] told me, "Just do Clint Eastwood." And so you'll be able to pick that out. (Bob Odenkirk)

show.title: The Big Lebowski
show.headline: The Coen Bros.' slacker odyssey really ties the room together

event.title: <show-title>The Big Lebowski</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: It's the greatest movie party ever, but that's just like, uh, our opinion, man
event.description:
You want a movie party? We can get you a movie party, believe me. There are ways, Dude. You don't wanna know about it, believe me.

With its endlessly quotable dialogue, twist-filled mystery, and iconic characters, THE BIG LEBOWSKI has become a fixture of modern pop culture. That’s why we’re putting together a movie party to celebrate the greatness that is El Duderino (we’re not into the whole “brevity” thing).

When you arrive, we’ll provide you with a "F**k It, Let's Go Bowling" bumper sticker to commemorate the forever-two-steps-behind Donny Kerabatsos, a notepad like Jackie Treehorn's, and rolling papers.

So throw on your favorite shabby sweater and roll on down for THE BIG LEBOWSKI Movie Party. It’ll be far out.

show.title: The Pitt
show.headline: Witness the season 2 finale of the hit HBO Max TV show

event.title: <show-title>The Pitt</show-title> <event-type-title>Free Victory Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Healthcare workers: see The Pitt season finale early & free
event.description:
HBO Max and WBTV invite healthcare workers to celebrate their steadfast dedication to their communities with a Free Victory Screening of the pulse-pounding Season 2 finale of The Pitt. All those who work at hospitals, clinics, urgent care, hospice, independent home advocates, healthcare professional retirees, and more are encouraged to reserve their seat with the purchase of a $10 food and beverage voucher.

All attendees will take home special The Pitt giveaways. The Pitt streams Thursdays, 9pm ET/6pm PT on HBO Max.

As this is an early screening, please do not reveal any plot points about the finale until after it streams on HBO Max, Thursday, April 16. We're counting on you to help preserve the experience for your fellow fans.

eventType.title: Free Victory Screening
eventType.headline: Our way of saying thank you to our Victory Members
eventType.description: Calling all Victory Members! We’re saying thanks with a free screening. Just buy a food & beverage voucher to reserve your seat, and then use it toward food and drink at the show.

show.title: Dance Freak
show.headline: A dangerous Dance Freak runs wild

event.title: <show-title>Dance Freak</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Live Q&A with directors Robby Rackleff and Alan Resnick
event.description:
In-person Q&A with Robby Rackleff and Alan Resnick following the screening

DANCE FREAK contains several sequences with flashing lights that may affect those who are susceptible to photosensitive epilepsy or have other photosensitivities.

In a secret government facility, a team of scientists has done impossible, indescribable things. Genetic mutations, inter-dimensional experiments – nasty top-secret stuff. They played GOD. And like GOD, they are all probably dead. The filmmakers only had $45,000 to make this movie, and they ran out of that money almost immediately, so a lot of that stuff is just implied through dialogue. There is dancing, but it isn’t that crazy. Also, the movie gets loud sometimes, but it's not that long.

show.title: Mermaid (2026)
show.headline: Florida man saves injured mermaid and nurses her back to health

event.title: <show-title>Mermaid (2026)</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Actor     Johnny Pemberton Live in Person
event.description:
In-person Q&A with actor/executive producer Johnny Pemberton following the screening.

Doug is an awkward Florida drug addict. Divorced and alone, he shares custody of his young daughter with his ex. Doug's reeling from losing his job at a local strip club, where he takes care of the fish in the aquarium. Things are beyond bleak, so Doug zips his boat out into the Gulf to end the pain of his reality. But at his lowest point, he discovers an injured mermaid creature clinging to life. For Doug, it's a sign from the Universe, so he brings her home, gives her shelter in his tub, and begins to nurse her back to health. He even names her Destiny. As their rapport blossoms, word of his secret gets out and he is forced to protect his new friend by any means necessary.

show.title: Jerry Maguire
show.headline: Tom Cruise had us at hello in the defining romantic comedy of the '90s

event.title: <show-title>Jerry Maguire</show-title> <event-type-title>30th Anniversary</event-type-title>
event.headline: Celebrate the 30th anniversary of Cameron Crowe's classic on the big screen
event.description: Tom Cruise was nominated for an Oscar® for his portrayal of a successful sports agent who is fired from a high-powered agency when he develops an honorable streak. To his dismay, he finds he has also lost most of his clients and his fiancée, publicist Avery Bishop (Kelly Preston). With only the least important client on his once-full roster, undersized wide receiver Rod Tidwell (Cuba Gooding Jr. in an Oscar®-winning performance), and the one person from his ex-firm who believes in him, wistful single mother Dorothy Boyd (Renée Zellweger), Jerry must start over and rebuild his life from the ground up. Nominated for five Academy Awards®, including Best Picture and Best Writing (Cameron Crowe, who also directed), with an outstanding cast including Bonnie Hunt, Jonathan Lipnicki, and Regina King.

show.title: Green Room
show.headline: Jeremy Saulnier's Punks vs. Nazis survivalist thriller.

event.title: <show-title>Green Room</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Jeremy Saulnier's Punks vs. Nazis survivalist thriller.
event.description:
With writer/director Jeremy Saulnier, schedule permitting

Down on their luck punk rockers The Ain’t Rights (Anton Yelchin, Imogene Poots, Alia Shawkat) are finishing up a long and unsuccessful tour, and are about to call it quits when they get an unexpected booking at an isolated, run-down club deep in the backwoods of Oregon. What seems merely to be a third-rate gig escalates into something much more sinister when they witness an act of violence backstage that they weren’t meant to see. Now trapped backstage, they must face off against the club’s depraved owner, Darcy Banker (Patrick Stewart), a man who will do anything to protect the secrets of his nefarious enterprise. But while Darcy and his henchmen think the band will be easy to get rid of, The Ain’t Rights prove themselves much more cunning and capable than anyone expected, turning the tables on their unsuspecting captors and setting the stage for the ultimate life-or-death showdown. (A24)

DCP courtesy of A24

show.title: The Getaway (1972)
show.headline: A recently-released ex-con and his wife go on the run after a heist goes awry

event.title: <show-title>The Getaway (1972)</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Bob Odenkirk and Ben Wheatley, ahead of their new film NORMAL
event.description:
THE GETAWAY (1972) is presented as part of our Bob Odenkirk and Ben Wheatley Guest Selects for their new film, NORMAL.

I chose this because I love Sam Peckinpah. It relates to NORMAL in that it's a contemporary crime film. The thing about THE GETAWAY for me is that it's so cold and so matter-of-fact – they're professionals going through the motions of the job. It's kind of restrained for Peckinpah as well. He doesn't have the absolute lunacy of, say, THE WILD BUNCH or BRING ME THE HEAD OF ALFREDO GARCIA. But I think it's the Jim Thompson-ness of it. It has a relentless kind of grimy crime momentum all the way through it. I think that's always in the back of my mind when I'm thinking about crime-thriller stuff. (Ben Wheatley)

show.title: Céline and Julie Go Boating
show.headline: Alice in Wonderland meets French New Wave

event.title: <show-title>Céline and Julie Go Boating</show-title>
event.headline: Jacques Rivette’s magical mystery tour of female friendship and whimsy
event.description:
“I always let myself be overcome by irrepressible waves of feeling.”

Does it ever feel like you and your closest friend live in your own little universe? Inside jokes no one else would understand, secret languages born of meaningless moments, the strange ability to communicate telepathically with a simple glance. Some relationships, romantic or platonic, have a mystical air, a touch of destiny. In Jacques Rivette's CÉLENE AND JULIE GO BOATING, magic brings two young women, Célene (Juliet Berto), a cabaret magician, and Julie (Dominique Labourier), a librarian, together and leads them down the rabbit hole, if you will, into a mysterious world of absurdity and *GASP* murder. To quote critic Beatrice Loayza, the pair engage in “play as a life force.” Célene and Julie take turns swapping identities, move in together (U-Hauling lol), and traverse time and space through a “house of fiction” with the help of magical candy…. There’s a lot going on here, and it’s all so very French!

A celebration of female friendship and “pleasure as a form of liberation,” CÉLENE AND JULIE GO BOATING is a remarkable film filled to the brim with imagination and disruption. Think BEING JOHN MALKOVICH with less existential dread, or MULHOLLAND DRIVE with less existential dread. Inspired by Alice in Wonderland, Věra Chytilová’s DAISIES, and the works of Marcel Proust and Jean Cocteau, CÉLENE AND JULIE GO BOATING brings whimsy and joyful femininity to the French New Wave, and it is a must-see for anyone who has ever wanted to follow an impulse beyond the threshold of expectation. It doesn’t hurt to fall off the moon.

show.title: Romancing in Thin Air
show.headline: Rarified love in Shangri-La

event.title: <show-title>Romancing in Thin Air</show-title>
event.headline: Rarified love in Shangri-La
event.description:
After being dumped publicly at the altar by his co-star, famous actor Michael (Louis Koo) falls into a drunken stupor that leads him to hide away in a truck that whisks him up into the snowy mountains of Shangri-La. The truck in question belongs to Sue (Sammi Cheng Sau-Man), a widow in denial that her late husband, who got lost in the woods nearby, is gone forever. As Michael starts healing himself in the tranquility of Sue’s quaint motel, he begins to fall in love with her.

ROMANCING IN THIN AIR director Johnnie To is most well known for his action and crime thrillers; his foray into melodramatic romance – a subgenre that is a staple in East Asian cinema – is touching, sweet, and deeply melancholic. Although To’s film definitely hits all the tropes that define romantic dramas, it’s also a love letter to storytelling and how cinema draws people to each other.

show.title: Evilspeak
show.headline: Remember the kittle kid you used to pick on? Well, he's a big boy now.

event.title: <show-title>Evilspeak</show-title>
event.headline: Remember the kittle kid you used to pick on? Well, he's a big boy now.
event.description:
It's a well-known fact that computers are Lucifer's Weapon of Mass Destruction in his continuing battle against the human race. But at the apex of mankind's evolution (aka 1981), ol' Beelzebub needed only a Commodore 64 and dot-matrix printer to unleash an apocalyptic storm of demonic possession and extermination. Legendary character actor Clint Howard (THE ICE CREAM MAN, THE WRAITH) stars in this harrowing, vicious tale of tortured military school dweeb Stanley. When pushed to the brink of human endurance by his bullying classmates and the drunken, rape-crazy elderly janitor ("I'm gonna show you how I turn little boys into little GIRLS!"), Stanley allies with an electronic incarnation of The Dark Lord to level the playing field. Featuring blazing decapitations, fountainous gore, man-eating Hell-hogs, and enough clergical dismemberment and digital anti-Christian imagery to forever reinforce the fact that Jesus Christ is a goddamn wimp. Hail Satan slash sideways smiley face dot wink. (Zack)

35mm courtesy of University of North Carolina School of the Arts

show.title: Communion
show.headline: One of the wildest performances in the Christopher Walken canon!

event.title: <show-title>Communion</show-title>
event.headline: One of the wildest performances in the Christopher Walken canon!
event.description:
“Even before the anal probing, he's nuts. From his first breath, he's out of his mind. His approach appears to be mostly ad-libbing. It's a combination of the Goldblum cadence with a touch of Shatner's Shakespearean stagger.” - Scumbalina, Letterboxd

One of the wildest performances in the Christopher Walken canon, and that’s really saying something! COMMUNION director Phillipe Mora (THE HOWLING II and III) explodes UFO guru Whitley Strieber’s foundational text into a watchable SNL parody-type version, perhaps unintentionally…but does it really matter? Here, Walken struts, dances and makes a mockery of our plane of existence and IT’S BEAUTIFUL. You will never, ever get the image of him beaming and cavorting with the little grey aliens out of your mind

DCP courtesy of Shout! Factory and the American Genre Film Archive

show.title: Mermaid (2026)
show.headline: Florida man saves injured mermaid and nurses her back to health

event.title: <show-title>Mermaid (2026)</show-title> <event-type-title>Sneak Preview</event-type-title>
event.headline: Be among the first to see this comedy about a Florida man who saves a mermaid
event.description: Doug is an awkward Florida drug addict. Divorced and alone, he shares custody of his young daughter with his ex. Doug's reeling from losing his job at a local strip club, where he takes care of the fish in the aquarium. Things are beyond bleak, so Doug zips his boat out into the Gulf to end the pain of his reality. But at his lowest point, he discovers an injured mermaid creature clinging to life. For Doug, it's a sign from the Universe, so he brings her home, gives her shelter in his tub, and begins to nurse her back to health. He even names her Destiny. As their rapport blossoms, word of his secret gets out and he is forced to protect his new friend by any means necessary.

event.description: When miserable couple Dan and Lisa retreat to a remote cabin for a romantic reset, each arrives with a secret plan to murder the other. As the toxic getaway spirals into chaotic carnage, Dan and Lisa must survive the intruders, each other, and figure out if they want to save their marriage or survive it.

show.title: F**k My Son! + Dance Freak
show.headline: A double feature of depravity

event.title: <show-title>F**k My Son! + Dance Freak</show-title> <event-type-title>Double Feature</event-type-title>
event.headline: A double feature of depravity
event.description:
DANCE FREAK contains several sequences with flashing lights that may affect those who are susceptible to photosensitive epilepsy or have other photosensitivities.

About FUCK MY SON!

Opening with a bargain-basement AMC theater pre-show complete with full-frontal dancing male nudity, Todd Rohal’s FUCK MY SON! adaptation is not for the faint of heart.

Expanding on Johnny Ryan’s underground comic of the same name, FUCK MY SON! is a tongue-in-cheek exploitation film in which everyone knows exactly what film they’re making.

It follows the plight of a young mother, out clothes shopping with her daughter Bernice, and teaching her hard life lessons as they catch a peeping tom watching them. If only that had been the hardest part of her day… Instead, the two try to help an old woman who’s fallen, only to quickly find the tables turned, as the old woman shoves the two in her van and kidnaps them to her secluded farmhouse.

Once there, with Bernice in a cage and the mother zip-tied to a pillar in a filthy basement, the old woman tries to make nice while she unveils her ultimate plan – to have the young woman fuck her son, at which point she’ll gladly let them go.

The nausea-inducing depravity that follows is balanced with a raunchy, bleak, absurdist humor, trademarks of both Johnny Ryan’s and Todd Rohal’s work.

Rohal is joined by long-time collaborators Steve Little and Robert Longstreet (as the old woman), along with an incredible performance from Tipper Newton as the young mother and a few other familiar faces along the way. The positively goopy practical makeup effects really bring this piece home and are provided by industry heavyweights Marcia King and Robert Kurtzman. All of this adds up to a not-to-be-missed spectacle. (Kev Dooley)

About DANCE FREAK

In a secret government facility, a team of scientists has done impossible, indescribable things. Genetic mutations, inter-dimensional experiments – nasty top-secret stuff. They played GOD. And like GOD, they are all probably dead. The filmmakers only had $45,000 to make this movie, and they ran out of that money almost immediately, so a lot of that stuff is just implied through dialogue. There is dancing, but it isn’t that crazy. Also, the movie gets loud sometimes, but it's not that long.

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.name: Baby Day Show
sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!

sessionAttributes.name: Kid Friendly
sessionAttributes.description: Grab the fam and let the fun begin! Our Kid Friendly screenings include pre-show activity sheets and crayons, a relaxed noise policy, and kids menus. Ages 3+ welcome with a parent or guardian.
